From: Evan Park <evan.park@cortexhealth.com>

Date: 2027-05-18T09:12:00Z
Subject: Capacity bump mid-term + co-term question

Hi Marissa,

Quick question on behalf of Cortex Health — our usage is ramping faster than forecast and we need to add ~1.5x GPU capacity starting June 1. We're currently mid-contract (ends 2028-01-15) and would prefer to co-term the new capacity to our existing renewal if possible.

Can you share: 1) a rough uplift % for adding capacity mid-contract (we'll do committed flat increase, not burst), 2) whether we can blend/annualize to co-term and how that affects the effective price, and 3) any one-time credits or pro-rata treatment you typically apply?

We'd like a ballpark this week to take to procurement.

Thanks,
Evan Park
Head of Cloud Ops
Cortex Health

Team,

We started seeing a small but growing set of r...
text:
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 04:12:00 +0000
Subject: Prefix-eviction anomaly causing tokenization mismatches — proposed emergency patch

Team,

We started seeing a small but growing set of requests that hit an early prefix-eviction path in the runtime which incorrectly trims the KV cache for long-conversation sessions. Symptoms: occasional tokenization mismatches and failed structured outputs for a subset of large-context chat sessions (p95 latency spike also observed). Root cause points to a regression in the eviction heuristic introduced in the recent scheduler update (sha: a1b2c3).

Impact: ~0.4% of requests across a small customer cohort (not all models). For Acme specifically, their test traffic shows repeated tracer IDs that triggered the path.

I have a...

Date: 2027-03-22T18:00:00-07:00
Subject: Recap: Fintronics call — logging scope, PII handling, and success criteria

Hi Ava — thanks again for your time earlier. Quick written recap so we have a single source of truth and clear next steps.

Short summary
- Pain: unpredictable inference costs and inability to reliably redact or segregate PII from logs.
- Current stack: on-prem inference layer + ELK for logs; data lake on S3; custom masking scripts run post-ingest.
- Goal: move to a predictable inference runtime, keep <200ms p95 for key endpoints, and implement auditable PII redaction with configurable retention (primary ask: 2 years for audit artifacts, 90 days for raw tokens).

Key discussion points (from call)
1) Redaction model: Fintronics needs redaction-at-ingest or re...

Hi Olga,

Thanks for engaging — we're ready to register Redwood as a vendor in our AP system. To move quickly our AP team needs:

- Official vendor/legal name to appear on the supplier record
- Company registration / VAT ID (for EU reverse-charge processing)
- Bank details (IBAN / BIC) and remit instructions
- Copies of any tax/VAT certificates you rely on (self-cert for reverse charge)
- Confirmation whether a customer PO will always be issued (we can enforce PO numbers)
- Preferred invoice file format (PDF, XML, CSV) and whether invoices can be submitted without a PO for subscription/usage billing

Hi Samir / Elena — thanks again for taking the time today. I wanted to capture the highlights and clear next steps so we’re all aligned.

Quick recap of what you told us:
- Primary goal: move ML inference for clinical triage scoring from proof-of-concept to a production pilot across 3 hospitals by Q2 2027.
- Current pain points: unpredictable latency during peak batch windows (evening batch jobs), ballooning per-token costs on long clinical notes, and lack of KV-cache reuse across similar requests.
- Workloads of interest: structured triage scoring (short prompts, high TPS)...
